# Bit by Bit Pedagogy

> Personalised academic coaching for medical students preparing for the USMLE (Step 1, Step 2 CK, Step 3) and COMLEX (Level 1, Level 2 CE). Methodology built around question-based learning, mechanism over memorisation, and behavioural consistency.

## Core methodology

- [The 4-Step Master Pivot](https://bitbybitpedagogy.com/method/master-pivot.html)
- [C/G/W Triage](https://bitbybitpedagogy.com/method/cgw-triage.html)
- [The Pivot Word / Differentiator Hunt](https://bitbybitpedagogy.com/method/pivot-word.html)
- [The Concept Anchor](https://bitbybitpedagogy.com/method/concept-anchor.html)
- [The 48-Hour Revisit](https://bitbybitpedagogy.com/method/48-hour-revisit.html)
- [The Concept Correction Log](https://bitbybitpedagogy.com/method/concept-correction-log.html)
- [Two Daily Systems](https://bitbybitpedagogy.com/method/two-daily-systems.html)
- [The Phased Roadmap](https://bitbybitpedagogy.com/method/phased-roadmap.html)
- [One Thing at a Time](https://bitbybitpedagogy.com/method/one-thing-at-a-time.html)

## Tools

- [Find My Gap (free 10-Q diagnostic)](https://bitbybitpedagogy.com/plan.html)
- [Concept Correction Log Generator](https://bitbybitpedagogy.com/log.html)

## Bit Library (65 reference pages)

Hub: https://bitbybitpedagogy.com/bits/

Each Bit covers one mechanism, one differentiator table, one NBME-style stem, one concept anchor.

- **Heme/Onc (8):** ITP/TTP/HUS/DIC · Hemophilia A/B/vWD · Iron/ACD/Thal · AML/ALL/CML/CLL · Hodgkin/NHL · Anemia by MCV · Intrinsic hemolytic (HS/G6PD/PNH/SCD) · MPNs (PV/ET/PMF/CML)
- **Renal (4):** Bartter/Gitelman/Liddle · Nephritic/Nephrotic · RTA 1/2/4 · AKI prerenal/intrinsic/postrenal
- **Endo (7):** DKA/HHS · Conn/Cushing/Pheo · Sheehan/Simmonds/Empty Sella · Hashimoto/Graves/De Quervain · T1DM/T2DM · Hyper/Hypoparathyroidism · MEN 1/2A/2B
- **Cardio (5):** AS vs HOCM · Restrictive vs Constrictive · HFrEF vs HFpEF · Cardiac murmurs · ACS (STEMI/NSTEMI/UA)
- **Neuro (6):** UMN vs LMN · Wernicke vs Korsakoff · MS/NMO/ADEM · Stroke syndromes · Dementia types · Movement disorders (Parkinson/ET/HD/Wilson)
- **Rheum/Immuno (5):** GPA/Goodpasture/EGPA · SLE/DIL/MCTD · Hypersensitivity I-IV · DiGeorge/Bruton/CVID/Hyper-IgM/SCID · CGD/Chédiak/Job/LAD
- **Genetics (3):** Prader-Willi/Angelman · Trisomy 13/18/21 · NF1/NF2/TSC/VHL
- **Biochem (5):** PKU/MSUD/Homocystinuria/Alkaptonuria · Marfan/Homocystinuria/EDS · Glycogen storage diseases · Lysosomal storage diseases · Vitamin deficiencies
- **Pathology (3):** Necrosis types · Apoptosis vs Necrosis · Oncogenes vs Tumor Suppressors
- **Micro (9):** Hepatitis A-E · Herpesviruses · Salmonella vs Shigella · Endotoxin vs Exotoxin · Exotoxin syndromes · HIV stages + OIs · TB stages · Dimorphic fungi · Plasmodium species
- **Pharm/Tox (5):** MUDPILES · HARDUP · Antibiotic mechanisms · Antiarrhythmics (Vaughan-Williams) · Diuretics by site
- **Behavioral (3):** Defense mechanisms · Personality disorders Clusters A/B/C · Mood disorders
- **GI (1):** Crohn vs UC
- **OB/Gyn (1):** OB bleeding emergencies (ectopic/molar/abruption/previa)

## Other reference content

- [High-Yield Differentiators hub](https://bitbybitpedagogy.com/differentiators.html)
- [10 NBME Trap Patterns](https://bitbybitpedagogy.com/nbme-traps.html)
- [50-Day Habit Reset](https://bitbybitpedagogy.com/habit-reset.html)

## Verification sources

Content paraphrased and verified against First Aid for the USMLE Step 1 2024 (34th ed), Robbins Basic Pathology 10e, Guyton & Hall Physiology 14e, Abbas Basic Immunology 6e, and Brenner & Stevens Pharmacology.
